openFindings.title: Cleanup script builds plan outside transaction (TOCTOU)
openFindings.evidence: const plan = buildPlan(database); const tx = database.transaction(() => applyCleanup(database, plan));

openFindings.title: FTS cleanup relies on assumed trigger with no runtime verification
openFindings.evidence: ftsCleanupStrategy: 'memory_index_trigger' - never asserted

openFindings.title: Symlink bypass: path.resolve does not follow symlinks
openFindings.evidence: canonicalFilePath = path.resolve(parsed.filePath).replace(/\\/g, '/')
openFindings.securityFraming: privilege-escalation: symlink in specs/ pointing to /external/ or /z_future/ bypasses invariant

openFindings.title: Cleanup LIKE patterns miss rows with no leading slash
openFindings.evidence: file_path LIKE '%/z_future/%' - diverges from (^|/)z_future(/|$) regex semantics
openFindings.securityFraming: cleanup-evasion: attacker storing relative z_future/ path bypasses cleanup while matching runtime regex

openFindings.title: Cleanup --apply has no idempotence short-circuit
openFindings.evidence: no early-exit branch when before summary shows zero violations

openFindings.title: stripDeletedIdsFromMutationLedger is a silent no-op stub
openFindings.evidence: function stripDeletedIdsFromMutationLedger(...): number { return 0; }

openFindings.title: Log-injection in save-time downgrade WARN (parser-controlled file_path logged verbatim)
openFindings.evidence: console.warn('[memory-save] ...', { file_path: parsed.filePath }) - no newline/control-char strip; parsed.filePath originates from frontmatter/caller input

openFindings.title: Silent tier downgrade still indexes attacker-declared-constitutional content; no governance audit row
openFindings.evidence: parsed.importanceTier = 'important'; save continues. No governance_audit insert on this branch. Silent downgrade conflicts with user-directed policy-grade invariant.

openFindings.title: Walker DoS: unbounded readdirSync + per-directory whole .gitignore read
openFindings.evidence: loadGitignore uses readFileSync with no size cap; findFiles.walk has no depth / node-count bound. Attacker controlling files in scanned dir can exhaust memory or block event loop.

openFindings.title: spec.md Status=In Progress contradicts verified delivered state
openFindings.evidence: spec.md Metadata row says 'In Progress' while impl-summary says 'Completed: Yes', checklist records Verification Date=2026-04-24, tasks.md completion_pct=100

openFindings.title: Stale _memory.continuity frontmatter in plan.md, decision-record.md, spec.md (completion_pct=20)
openFindings.evidence: All three files carry completion_pct=20 and scaffolding-era recent_action strings; tasks.md/checklist.md/impl-summary.md were updated to completion_pct=100. Resume ladder reads these blocks verbatim.

openFindings.title: plan.md Implementation Phases + DoD checkboxes remain unchecked after completion
openFindings.evidence: Definition of Done has three [ ] items; Phases 1-3 all [ ] unchecked; tasks.md T001-T014 all [x]; checklist.md CHK-001..CHK-122 all [x]

openFindings.title: isConstitutionalPath recompiles regex per call
README.md rejection is defense-in-depth by coincidence
openFindings.securityFraming: future PR adding readme.md to SPEC_DOCUMENT_FILENAMES_SET silently opens constitutional ingestion path

openFindings.title: Two guard invocations with different input normalization
EXCLUDED_FOR_CODE_GRAPH blanket-excludes any 'dist' segment
Cleanup collectSummary.zFutureRows mixes OR conditions that delete cannot match
Cleanup console.error leaks absolute DB path on better-sqlite3 I/O failure
openFindings.evidence: console.error('[cleanup-index-scope-violations] Error:', error.message) - better-sqlite3 embeds absolute DB path in error messages

openFindings.title: Cleanup produces no machine-readable report
openFindings.evidence: printSummary/printApplySummary emit key=value lines, no JSON option. Downstream compliance-audit pipelines cannot parse reliably.

openFindings.title: Supply-chain: sqlite-vec native extension loaded unconditionally in cleanup
openFindings.evidence: loadSqliteVec(database) loads native extension with no integrity check; compromised npm package executes attacker native code against live DB.

openFindings.title: spec.md Section 13 open-question about README count (2 vs 3) is stale after ADR-005
openFindings.evidence: Open question text still lists 2-or-3 ambiguity; ADR-005 resolves this to 2 unambiguously (README excluded). Should be removed or annotated RESOLVED.

openFindings.title: feature_catalog and manual_testing_playbook carry no entry for the three invariants or index-scope helper
openFindings.evidence: grep for z_future/external/constitutional-tier/index-scope returns only unrelated hits. Not in declared spec.md Files-to-Change scope, but compounds with packet age.

openFindings.title: Decision-record does not document packet 010/011 composition for save-path defense-in-depth
openFindings.evidence: ADR-001 Constraints mentions scope-lock against packet 010 fromScan work but no ADR explains how 011's save-time guard layers with 010's canonical-path post-filter (Fix A) + fromScan transactional recheck (Fix B2). Reviewers cannot reconstruct layering from either packet alone.

openFindings.title: Parent 026 graph-metadata.json child reference contains double-slash drift
openFindings.evidence: Path 'system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization//011-index-scope-and-constitutional-tier-invariants' has '//' between parent and child slug; adjacent description.json:41 uses single-slash convention.

openFindings.title: Parallel exclusion lists in spec-doc-paths.ts duplicate index-scope.ts invariants
openFindings.evidence: SPEC_DOCUMENT_EXCLUDED_SEGMENTS carries /z_archive/ while EXCLUDED_FOR_MEMORY also carries z_archive. Two sources of truth for the same invariant, no documentation of the layering contract.

openFindings.title: SPEC_DOC_EXCLUDE_DIRS in memory-index-discovery.ts is a third exclusion source with partial overlap and 'iterations' granularity drift
openFindings.evidence: new Set(['scratch', 'memory', 'node_modules', 'iterations', 'z_archive']) overlaps both EXCLUDED_FOR_MEMORY and SPEC_DOCUMENT_EXCLUDED_SEGMENTS; bare 'iterations' diverges from '/research/iterations/' + '/review/iterations/' granularity in spec-doc-paths.ts.

openFindings.title: No code back-references to packet 011, ADR-004, or ADR-005 anywhere in shipped implementation
openFindings.evidence: Grep for 'packet 011', '011-index-scope', 'ADR-004', 'ADR-005' across all four touched files returns zero hits. A future maintainer refactoring the soft-downgrade branch has no local signal that it enforces a user-directed invariant.

openFindings.title: Cleanup script diverges from sibling-script conventions (DB_PATH source, banners)
openFindings.evidence: Sibling uses import { DB_PATH } from '@spec-kit/shared/paths'; new script uses inline fileURLToPath(new URL('../../../mcp_server/database/context-index__voyage__voyage-4__1024.sqlite', import.meta.url)). If embedding model rotates, sibling follows automatically; new script silently opens non-existent path.

openFindings.title: No test-visible access to compileSegmentPattern / normalizeIndexScopePath / isConstitutionalPath
openFindings.evidence: Tests import only the top-level shouldIndex* functions + EXCLUDED_* arrays; isConstitutionalPath has zero direct unit tests; substring boundary tests (constitutional-reference, not_constitutional) absent.

openFindings.title: index-scope.ts has no JSDoc on any exported function
openFindings.evidence: Three exported functions (shouldIndexForMemory, shouldIndexForCodeGraph, isConstitutionalPath) have zero JSDoc. Consumers must read the regex arrays to understand the promise.

openFindings.title: Tests do not lock in that readme.md is excluded under /constitutional/
openFindings.evidence: No test asserts isMemoryFile('/workspace/.opencode/skill/foo/constitutional/readme.md') === false. ADR-005 is user-directed but unguarded by a regression test.

openFindings.title: Operator-facing --verify has no CI hook documented
openFindings.evidence: --verify exit-code contract (0 clean, 1 violations) is documented in help text but no CI config, package.json script, or README line references it. DB drift goes undetected between releases.

openFindings.title: Function naming: shouldIndexForMemory ambiguous with RAM vs memory-system-index
openFindings.evidence: 'Memory' in this codebase can mean (a) canonical memory-system index or (b) in-process RAM. Symmetric pair works only if reader knows shorthand; shouldIndexForMemorySystem / shouldIndexForCodeGraphSystem would remove ambiguity.
